content: Whose son was Zephaniah?
explanation: Zephaniah 1:1 — 'The word of the LORD that came to Zephaniah the son of Cushi, son of Gedaliah, son of Amariah, son of Hezekiah...' The four-generation genealogy back to King Hezekiah suggests a royal lineage.

content: During which king of Judah did Zephaniah prophesy?
explanation: Zephaniah 1:1 — '...in the days of Josiah the son of Amon, king of Judah.' Josiah was the reforming king (2 Kings 22-23); Zephaniah may have supported his reforms.

content: According to Zephaniah 1:7, what is the reason to be silent before the Lord GOD?
explanation: Zephaniah 1:7 — 'Be silent before the Lord GOD! For the day of the LORD is near...'
options: Because the Day of the LORD is near

content: According to Zephaniah 1:12, what do the complacent say in their hearts?
explanation: Zephaniah 1:12 — 'At that time I will search Jerusalem with lamps, and I will punish the men who are complacent... those who say in their hearts, "The LORD will not do good, nor will he do ill."' Spiritual apathy — believing God neither cares nor acts.
options: God is only merciful, never judging
The LORD will not do good, nor will He do ill

content: According to Zephaniah 1:14, how is the great day of the LORD described?
explanation: Zephaniah 1:14 — 'The great day of the LORD is near, near and hastening fast...' The repetition 'near, near' emphasizes urgency.

content: How does Zephaniah 1:15 describe the Day of the LORD?
explanation: Zephaniah 1:15 — 'A day of wrath is that day, a day of distress and anguish, a day of ruin and devastation, a day of darkness and gloom, a day of clouds and thick darkness.'
options: A day of peace and feasting
A day of wrath, distress, anguish, and darkness
A day of grace and universal deliverance
A day of quietness and forgiveness

content: According to Zephaniah 1:18, what cannot deliver them from God's wrath?
explanation: Zephaniah 1:18 — 'Neither their silver nor their gold shall be able to deliver them on the day of the wrath of the LORD...'

content: According to Zephaniah 2:3, what three things must the humble seek?
explanation: Zephaniah 2:3 — 'Seek the LORD, all you humble of the land, who do his just commands; seek righteousness; seek humility...' Three 'seek' commands in one verse — a classic prophetic pattern.

content: According to Zephaniah 2:13, which land will the LORD make desolate?
explanation: Zephaniah 2:13 — 'And he will stretch out his hand against the north and destroy Assyria, and he will make Nineveh a desolation...'

content: According to Zephaniah 3:12, what kind of people will the LORD leave among His people?
explanation: Zephaniah 3:12 — 'But I will leave in your midst a people humble and lowly. They shall seek refuge in the name of the LORD.' Humble and lowly — bowed down, relying wholly on the LORD.
options: Humble and lowly, seeking refuge in the name of the LORD

content: According to Zephaniah 1:2, the LORD will utterly sweep away everything from where?
explanation: Zephaniah 1:2 — 'I will utterly sweep away everything from the face of the earth, declares the LORD.' Language akin to the Flood — total judgment.
options: From the face of the earth
tags: Sweep from the face of the earth

content: According to Zephaniah 3:9, what will God give to the peoples?
explanation: Zephaniah 3:9 — 'For at that time I will change the speech of the peoples to a pure speech, that all of them may call upon the name of the LORD and serve him with one accord.' A prophecy of the nations worshiping God — reversing Babel.
options: A pure speech to call upon the name of the LORD

content: According to Zephaniah 3:13, what will the remnant of Israel NOT do?
explanation: Zephaniah 3:13 — 'Those who are left in Israel; they shall do no injustice and speak no lies, nor shall there be found in their mouth a deceitful tongue...' Character transformed — no injustice, lies, or deceit.
options: Seek the LORD and worship Him
Teach the law to their children

content: Whom does Zephaniah 3:14 call to sing for joy?
explanation: Zephaniah 3:14 — 'Sing aloud, O daughter of Zion; shout, O Israel! Rejoice and exult with all your heart, O daughter of Jerusalem!'
options: Daughter of Zion, Israel, daughter of Jerusalem

content: According to Zephaniah 3:15, where is the King of Israel — the LORD?
explanation: Zephaniah 3:15 — '...The King of Israel, the LORD, is in your midst; you shall never again fear evil.' God's presence is the security of His people.
options: In the house of the prophets

content: According to Zephaniah 3:17, the LORD your God in your midst is what kind of One who 'will save'?
explanation: Zephaniah 3:17 — 'The LORD your God is in your midst, a mighty one who will save; he will rejoice over you with gladness...'

content: According to Zephaniah 3:17, what does God do 'over you with gladness'?
explanation: Zephaniah 3:17 — '...he will rejoice over you with gladness; he will quiet you by his love; he will exult over you with loud singing.' A unique image — God sings over His people.

content: According to Zephaniah 2:3, what must the humble of the land seek? (select all that apply)
explanation: Zephaniah 2:3 — 'Seek the LORD, all you humble of the land... seek righteousness; seek humility...' Three things to seek; 'seek wealth and power' contradicts the passage.

content: According to Zephaniah 1:15, the Day of the LORD is described by which pairs of images? (select all that apply)
explanation: Zephaniah 1:15 — 'A day of wrath... a day of distress and anguish, a day of ruin and devastation, a day of darkness and gloom, a day of clouds and thick darkness.' Four bleak pairs; 'day of joy and wedding feast' contradicts.
options: Darkness and gloom, clouds and thick darkness
tags: Marks of the Day of LORD

content: According to Zephaniah 3:17, what does God do for His people? (select all that apply)
explanation: Zephaniah 3:17 — '...he will rejoice over you with gladness; he will quiet you by his love; he will exult over you with loud singing.' Three actions; 'rebuke without ceasing' contradicts the flow of love in this verse.
options: Exult over you with loud singing
Rebuke you without ceasing for every fault
